principles: Treat policy as a versioned, source-aware, evidence-backed governance artifact, not just searchable text.
Use PostgreSQL as the canonical store; derive search indexes, graph views, exports, and UI representations from canonical records.
Separate source, raw artifact, logical document, version, section, citation, relationship, obligation, finding, review, and export objects.
Every inferred relationship, obligation, gap, or impact claim requires evidence locator, confidence score, trust label, creator, and review state.
Prefer official and machine-readable sources, but preserve raw artifacts and source metadata for reproducibility.

assessment.headline: Represent echelon 3 and 4 as a local governance bundle, not a single document type.
notes: The current candidates are strong as anchor nodes, but the model should distinguish command policy, operating procedure, cyber control narrative, technical baseline, evidence, waiver, tasking, and review artifacts.
Echelon 3 is usually where service or major-command policy becomes command-executable governance: command instructions, supplements, program guides, control matrices, and regional policy.

Echelon 4 is usually where governance becomes proof-bearing local execution: SOPs, watchbills, system security plan control statements, baselines, scan outputs, POA&Ms, SARs, authorization decisions, exceptions, and retained evidence.

examples: fleet, TYCOM, major command, region, center, wing, or command instruction
artifactCandidates.bestUse: Best representation for formal local authority that subordinate units must follow.
Best representation for how higher policy is narrowed or operationalized without replacing it.
Best representation for traceability from policy statement to system/control implementation.
Best representation for how a command or activity actually performs the governed work.
Best representation for the local system-level assertion that a requirement is implemented, inherited, planned, or not applicable.
Best representation for the testable technical state that proves or challenges a control narrative.
Best representation for whether local implementation is verified, deficient, waived, accepted, or awaiting action.
Best representation for execution state after a governance requirement becomes work.

sourceRoleTaxonomy.purpose: Defines binding or quasi-binding governance language that can create obligations, delegated authorities, applicability, or supersession chains.
Shows how a higher authority is translated into doctrine, procedures, forms, controls, tasks, command instructions, or system-level requirements.
Provides early warning, context, funding, acquisition, or lifecycle movement that may imply policy pressure but usually needs reconciliation before becoming an authority edge.
Captures whether a requirement was adopted, implemented, assessed, accepted, remediated, or assigned to an accountable owner.

discoverySurfaces.gap: These sources may stop above the local command or hide CUI/classified items behind authenticated portals.

discoverySurfaces.trust: Authoritative for time-bound service implementation signals
discoverySurfaces.access: Public indexes plus archived/request-only or non-public message traffic
discoverySurfaces.gap: Messages can create urgent obligations or cancel prior messages, but may not be the durable parent directive; link them to DONI/MCPEL or local implementation artifacts when they announce formal policy.

discoverySurfaces.surface: Defense agency and component issuance systems
discoverySurfaces.gap: Agency policy often carries delegated authority for a domain such as logistics, networks, health, or industrial security. Capture the parent DoD authority, component scope, and whether the source is public or access-controlled.

discoverySurfaces.trust: Operational doctrine and professional practice authority
discoverySurfaces.gap: Doctrine may not create a compliance obligation by itself, but it explains how missions interpret policy. Model doctrine as implementation context unless a parent policy explicitly makes it mandatory.

locations.label: OUSD(A&S) Defense Pricing and Contracting Policy Vault

discoverySurfaces.gap: These sources often explain why a program or system is changing. Link them to Program, Requirement, FundingLine, Deadline, and Opportunity objects before treating them as direct policy authority.

discoverySurfaces.surface: Standards, forms, and logistics reference systems
discoverySurfaces.trust: Authoritative reference or required implementation artifact
discoverySurfaces.gap: Standards and forms frequently sit below policy but above local execution. Capture the requiring authority, version, applicability, and whether a form or standard is merely referenced or mandated.

discoverySurfaces.trust: Authoritative when hosted by the command

locations.label: Naval Education and Training Command resources

discoverySurfaces.gap: Coverage is uneven; some command libraries are public, some are stale, and many local artifacts are not public.
These identify what must be implemented or checked, but not whether a specific local system actually implemented it.
Discovery requires connectors, access governance, CUI marking, and source owner validation.
Evidence proves a system or asset state; it must be linked back to policy authority through control mappings and locators.

endpoints: Navy.mil ALNAV resources as secondary discovery
sourceObjectMappings.caveat: Use NAVADMIN as a message artifact, not automatically as the durable directive. Reconcile to DONI/OPNAV/SECNAV if it references a formal issuance.
DONI is the stronger source for durable Navy policy; messages are often implementation or announcement artifacts around it.
Some directive records are index-only or restricted; model as guarded SourceEndpoint rather than missing authority.

endpoints: command administrative publication sites for supplements
sourceObjectMappings.caveat: APD is Army-wide; echelon 3/4 command regulations often live on command publication portals and must cite APD parents.

sourceObjectMappings.source: Department of the Air Force e-Publishing
sourceObjectMappings.caveat: DAF publications can cover Air Force and Space Force; capture applicability and supplement chain explicitly.
Separate formal publications from message traffic; link MARADMIN/ALMAR to MCO/MCBUL when one implements or changes the other.
Component authority can be narrower than service authority but deeper inside a mission domain. Always capture the component, delegated scope, and parent DoD issuance.
DLMS records are unusually schema-like. Model data elements and transaction conventions separately from narrative policy sections.
Doctrine is usually implementation context rather than compliance authority. Promote to binding obligation only when another artifact incorporates it by reference.
These are often signal or program governance records. The model should connect them to authority and implementation, not collapse them into one policy artifact.
A form or standard may be cited, adopted, prescribed, or merely referenced. Those should be separate relationship types.
This is the main echelon 3/4 discovery zone. Treat stale public pages and duplicated SharePoint copies as source-quality issues, not duplicate authorities.
Proof sources should never become parent policy. Their power is traceability from evidence back to the authority chain and forward to review state.

sourceFamilies.name: GovInfo
sourceFamilies.accessMode: API, bulk, package download
sourceFamilies.notes: GovInfo package/granule structure is a strong fit for RawArtifact to ArtifactVersion to ArtifactSection decomposition.

sourceFamilies.name: Congress.gov API
sourceFamilies.accessMode: API
sourceFamilies.notes: Use for upstream legislative status and bill-to-public-law identity resolution. Use GovInfo for authenticated packages when available.

sourceFamilies.name: Federal Register
sourceFamilies.accessMode: API, RSS, HTML, PDF, XML
sourceFamilies.notes: Use as publication/legal notice layer and a bridge to Regulations.gov dockets.

sourceFamilies.name: Regulations.gov
sourceFamilies.accessMode: API
sourceFamilies.notes: Use to connect Federal Register rulemaking records to dockets, comments, supporting evidence, and public participation windows.

sourceFamilies.name: eCFR
sourceFamilies.accessMode: API and HTML
sourceFamilies.notes: Use as canonical regulatory section source for exact CFR citations and crosswalks.

sourceFamilies.name: OMB Memoranda and Guidance
sourceFamilies.accessMode: HTML index, PDF
sourceFamilies.notes: High-value implementation guidance, often bridges executive direction to agency action and acquisition/cyber obligations.

sourceFamilies.name: White House Presidential Actions
sourceFamilies.accessMode: HTML, RSS/sitemap where available
sourceFamilies.notes: Use for early executive signal; reconcile against Federal Register for official publication metadata.

sourceFamilies.name: DoD/WHS Issuances
sourceFamilies.accessMode: HTML index, PDF
sourceFamilies.notes: Core defense policy corpus. Parser should prioritize reference sections, applicability, responsibilities, and lifecycle statements.

sourceFamilies.name: Service and Component Source Registry
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Roll-up registry; resolve into component-specific endpoints
sourceFamilies.notes: Roll-up row only. The concrete SourceEndpoint should be DONI, MyNavyHR messages, JCS library, APD, DAF e-Publishing, Marines.mil messages, MCPEL, or a command-owned portal.

sourceFamilies.name: CJCS / Joint Staff Directives Library
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public HTML/PDF for unrestricted; .mil/.gov or SIPR for limited releasability
sourceFamilies.notes: Use as the joint policy layer between DoD issuances and service/component implementation. Limited releasability creates guarded SourceEndpoint variants.

sourceFamilies.name: Department of the Navy Issuances
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public SharePoint-style HTML library, PDF
sourceFamilies.notes: Primary source for SECNAV/OPNAV artifacts. Use directive number and PDF hash as stable identity; parse references and cancellation language aggressively.

sourceFamilies.name: MyNavyHR NAVADMIN / ALNAV Messages
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public HTML yearly indexes and TXT/HTML message files; older archives by request
sourceFamilies.notes: This is where NAVADMINs come from. Treat NAVADMIN/ALNAV as message artifacts with short lifecycle and high implementation signal; reconcile with DONI when messages modify or announce formal directives.

sourceFamilies.name: Marines.mil MARADMIN / ALMAR Messages
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public searchable HTML message index
sourceFamilies.notes: Marine Corps analog to Navy message traffic. Strong source for implementation guidance, cancellations, reminders, board announcements, and time-bound obligations.

sourceFamilies.name: Marine Corps Publications Electronic Library
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public HTML/PDF publication library
sourceFamilies.notes: Use for formal USMC orders/directives; link MARADMIN/ALMAR messages to this when messages announce, cancel, or implement formal publications.

sourceFamilies.name: Army Publishing Directorate
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public publication and form index, PDF
sourceFamilies.notes: Primary source for Army-wide formal publications. Command-level regulations and supplements may live on command publication sites and should point back to APD records.

sourceFamilies.name: Department of the Air Force e-Publishing
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public publication and form index, PDF
sourceFamilies.notes: Primary source for Air Force and Space Force publications. Supplements and guidance memoranda are important propagation edges to echelon 2/3 implementation.

sourceFamilies.name: Defense Agency / Component Issuance Registry
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Roll-up discovery surface; resolve to agency-specific public or guarded endpoints
sourceFamilies.notes: Use as a component discovery layer, then instantiate concrete DLA, DISA, DHA, DCSA, DCMA, or other agency SourceEndpoint records.

sourceFamilies.name: DLA Public Issuances
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public PDF plus CAC-only DLA issuance variants
sourceFamilies.notes: DLA-specific authority for logistics and agency operations. Treat CAC-only listings as guarded SourceEndpoint records with public metadata when available.

sourceFamilies.name: Defense Data Standards / DLMS Publications
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public HTML/PDF manuals, approved changes, and data tables
sourceFamilies.notes: Best source for logistics business rules and machine-readable implementation vocabulary beneath DoD supply chain policy.

sourceFamilies.name: DISA Policy Publication Information
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public publication index plus limited-distribution DISA policy
sourceFamilies.notes: Model separately from Cyber Exchange STIGs: DISA publications are agency/network governance, while STIGs/SRGs are technical implementation baselines.

sourceFamilies.name: DHA / Health.mil Publications
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public searchable publication center, PDF
sourceFamilies.notes: Health.mil is the public publication surface for DHA/MHS artifacts. Preserve medical, privacy, and clinical applicability scopes distinctly from generic command policy.

sourceFamilies.name: DCSA Publications and Industrial Security Guidance
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public pages and PDFs plus controlled industrial security systems
sourceFamilies.notes: Use for industrial security, clearance, insider threat, and DIB-facing implementation context; link back to NISPOM, CFR, DoD issuances, and contract security clauses.

sourceFamilies.name: Joint Doctrine / Joint Electronic Library
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public JCS doctrine pages plus JEL+ gated library for some records
sourceFamilies.notes: Doctrine explains operational interpretation. Treat as implementation context unless an authority artifact explicitly mandates compliance with the doctrine.

sourceFamilies.name: Air Force Doctrine
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public doctrine library
sourceFamilies.notes: Adds Air Force operational doctrine as a peer to DAF e-Publishing policy. Use applicability to keep doctrine from being confused with compliance directives.

sourceFamilies.name: Navy Warfare / NWDC Doctrine Libraries
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Mixed public, CAC-gated, and operational libraries
sourceFamilies.notes: Important for echelon 3/4 operational interpretation, but many authoritative doctrine libraries are gated. Model source presence and access controls even when content cannot be ingested.

sourceFamilies.name: DAU Adaptive Acquisition Framework
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public web knowledge base with linked policy references
sourceFamilies.notes: Useful for program governance and pathway-aware tasking. Reconcile AAF pages to the underlying DoDI, CJCSI, or memo before assigning binding authority.

sourceFamilies.name: JCIDS / CJCSI 5123 Materials
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public CJCS directives plus controlled JCIDS artifacts and SIPR references
sourceFamilies.notes: Critical bridge between law/policy and program requirements. Treat CDD/ICD/IS-CDD and JROC decisions as program governance artifacts with access-controlled evidence.

sourceFamilies.name: OUSD(A&S) / DPC Policy Vault
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public policy vault and memo archive
sourceFamilies.notes: Often creates acquisition implementation pressure before formal issuance updates. Mark as memo/signal unless it states binding class deviation or delegated authority.

sourceFamilies.name: OUSD(C) Budget Materials
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public budget justification materials and exhibits
contains: program acquisition cost by weapon system
sourceFamilies.notes: Budget materials are not policy by themselves, but they reveal resourced implementation, modernization priorities, and program-level effects of governance changes.

sourceFamilies.name: ASSIST / Defense Standardization Program
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public quick search plus registered ASSIST access for advanced records
sourceFamilies.notes: Official standards corpus. Distinguish cited standard, adopted standard, specification, and data item description because each creates different relationship edges.

sourceFamilies.name: DoD / Component Forms Repositories
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public forms index plus component forms libraries
sourceFamilies.notes: Forms are concrete implementation artifacts. Link each form to its prescribing directive and to evidence records where completed forms prove execution.

sourceFamilies.name: Command Publication Libraries
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Public, CAC-gated, SharePoint, Teams, Intelink, or local file library
sourceFamilies.notes: Primary source family for echelon 3/4 governance artifacts. Needs source owner validation, stale-content handling, and CUI/classification metadata.

sourceFamilies.name: Operational Evidence Systems
sourceFamilies.accessMode: Restricted APIs, exports, connectors, and evidence repositories
sourceFamilies.notes: These systems prove adoption and operational state. Every record needs lineage back to a control, local policy statement, system, asset, and source locator.

sourceFamilies.name: NIST CSRC Publications
sourceFamilies.accessMode: HTML search, PDF, data files where available
sourceFamilies.notes: Use for control and cybersecurity taxonomy, especially mappings to FedRAMP, FISMA, CUI, and agency security requirements.

sourceFamilies.name: DoD Cyber Exchange / DISA STIGs and SRGs
sourceFamilies.accessMode: HTML index, ZIP, XCCDF, CKL/CKLB, PDF where available
sourceFamilies.notes: Use as technical implementation layer that turns higher policy and control mappings into testable configuration rules, scan findings, checklists, and exception evidence.

sourceFamilies.name: DISN Connection Process Guide
sourceFamilies.accessMode: HTML guidance and templates
sourceFamilies.notes: Use for network/enclave connection artifacts; this is a better representation than a generic network statement because it identifies topology, approval, sponsor, system, and security package evidence.

sourceFamilies.name: CISA Directives, Alerts, and KEV
sourceFamilies.accessMode: HTML, JSON/CSV feeds where available
sourceFamilies.notes: Critical for operational cyber obligations and deadline extraction. KEV entries map naturally to Deadline and ImplementationEvidence objects.

sourceFamilies.name: Acquisition.gov FAR
sourceFamilies.accessMode: HTML, DITA, PDF, Word, ePub
sourceFamilies.notes: Use for acquisition policy context and clause/solicitation requirement crosswalks.

sourceFamilies.name: Acquisition.gov DFARS
sourceFamilies.accessMode: HTML, DITA, PDF, Word
sourceFamilies.notes: Defense-specific acquisition policy bridge to SAM.gov opportunities and downstream contract requirements.

sourceFamilies.name: SAM.gov Contract Opportunities
sourceFamilies.accessMode: API, bulk data, UI search
sourceFamilies.notes: Downstream opportunity records should never create policy authority, but can confirm market impact or implementation demand.

sourceFamilies.name: SAM.gov Contract Awards
sourceFamilies.accessMode: API
sourceFamilies.notes: Useful for validating whether policy-driven opportunities turned into market activity.

sourceFamilies.name: Grants.gov
sourceFamilies.accessMode: REST API, SOAP for some system-to-system flows
sourceFamilies.notes: Adds non-contract funding signals and policy-to-grant impact paths.

sourceFamilies.name: FedRAMP Documentation and Marketplace
sourceFamilies.accessMode: HTML, PDF, templates, spreadsheets
sourceFamilies.notes: Use as evidence and implementation-model corpus, not statutory authority. Strong bridge to NIST and agency cloud requirements.

sourceFamilies.name: Internal or Restricted Repositories
sourceFamilies.accessMode: guarded connector
sourceFamilies.notes: Metadata-only until guarded processing, classification, and export controls are approved.

entities.name: SourceRegistry
entities.purpose: Approved or candidate publication source with trust, access, cadence, parser, owner, and freshness controls.

entities.name: SourceEndpoint
entities.purpose: Concrete URL/API/feed/document index monitored under a source.

entities.name: SourceRun
entities.purpose: Execution record for monitor/fetch/ingest work against one source.

entities.name: RawArtifact
entities.purpose: Immutable fetched payload or file preserved exactly as received.

entities.name: ParsedArtifact
entities.purpose: Parser output bundle for a raw artifact, including extracted text, metadata, tables, and locators.

entities.name: ParserProfile
entities.purpose: Versioned parser strategy for a source family and file format.

entities.name: IntelligenceObject
entities.purpose: Shared graphable base object for artifacts, orgs, events, opportunities, obligations, findings, and evidence.
relationships: is extended by GovernanceArtifact, Organization, Program, Event, Opportunity, Finding, Obligation

entities.name: GovernanceArtifact
entities.purpose: Logical policy/directive/guidance/document family independent of a specific version or file.

entities.name: ArtifactVersion
entities.purpose: Specific issue/version/revision/change package of a governance artifact.

entities.name: ArtifactSection
entities.purpose: Addressable structural unit with locator and text hash.

entities.name: ArtifactIdentifier
entities.purpose: All identifiers attached to an artifact, with one canonical identifier.

entities.name: ArtifactLifecycleEvent
entities.purpose: Version and status event such as issued, amended, superseded, cancelled, rescinded, expired, or reissued.

entities.name: Organization
entities.purpose: Publisher, owner, sponsor, affected organization, reviewer organization, or implementation party.

entities.name: PolicyRoleAssignment
entities.purpose: Role a person, office, or organization has within a policy artifact.

entities.name: ArtifactApplicability
entities.purpose: Scope statement defining who/what is included or excluded.
relationships: may point to Organization, Program, CapabilityArea, MissionArea

entities.name: CapabilityArea
entities.purpose: Policy-relevant capability taxonomy used for filters, impact, and opportunity mapping.

entities.name: TaxonomyTerm
entities.purpose: Reusable controlled term from NIST, acquisition, cyber, mission, or internal taxonomies.

entities.name: Citation
entities.purpose: Raw or normalized citation extracted from section text, metadata, footnotes, or reference lists.

entities.name: CitationResolution
entities.purpose: Resolved target object for a citation with match evidence and confidence.

entities.name: EntityRelationship
entities.purpose: Canonical typed edge between any two graphable objects.
relationships: can be direct, inferred, or reviewed

entities.name: RelationshipType
entities.purpose: Allowed edge ontology with directionality and object-type constraints.

entities.name: EvidenceLocator
entities.purpose: Stable pointer to the evidence supporting a field, relationship, obligation, finding, or review decision.

entities.name: Obligation
entities.purpose: Extracted actor/action/object/modal requirement, usually from shall/must/will/should language.

entities.name: Deadline
entities.purpose: Absolute or relative timing constraint tied to an obligation, source event, opportunity, or review item.

entities.name: ImplementationEvidence
entities.purpose: Downstream proof, package, plan, report, control implementation, or artifact showing action against an obligation.

entities.name: Finding
entities.purpose: Reviewable gap, risk, conflict, stale-reference, lifecycle, or impact finding.
relationships: may link to Opportunity or Event

entities.name: Insight
entities.purpose: Source-grounded summary, why-it-matters note, trend, or impact card.
relationships: summarizes GovernanceArtifact, Relationship, Event, Opportunity, or Finding

entities.name: Event
entities.purpose: Conference, hearing, webinar, briefing, meeting, or announcement with policy relevance.

entities.name: Opportunity
entities.purpose: Contract, grant, SBIR/STTR, OTA, BAA, RFI, or forecast opportunity.

entities.name: CrossSurfaceLink
entities.purpose: Evidence-bearing connection between policy objects and events/opportunities/programs.
relationships: specialized EntityRelationship for Events and Opportunities surfaces

entities.name: Program
entities.purpose: Mission, acquisition, platform, system, or implementation program affected by policy.

entities.name: ReviewTask
entities.purpose: Human-in-the-loop review item for ambiguous, inferred, high-impact, or restricted outputs.

entities.name: ReviewDecision
entities.purpose: Reviewer outcome, rationale, and publishability change.

entities.name: AgentDefinition
entities.purpose: Controlled worker declaration with mission, inputs, outputs, model/rules, guardrails, metrics, and UX surface.

entities.name: AgentRun
entities.purpose: Runtime execution record for an agent.

entities.name: APIContract
entities.purpose: Versioned schema/export/API contract with deterministic ordering and QA status.

entities.name: ExportJob
entities.purpose: Deterministic export request and generated artifact manifest.

relationshipOntology.evidenceRequirement: Direct citation, authority statement, reference list, or official metadata preferred.
relationshipOntology.publishRule: Can publish direct evidence after contract checks; inferred authority requires review.

relationshipOntology.evidenceRequirement: Lifecycle language in source text or official metadata with locator.
relationshipOntology.publishRule: High review priority because lifecycle mistakes alter status and trust.

relationshipOntology.evidenceRequirement: Raw citation text plus resolved target candidate and confidence.
relationshipOntology.publishRule: Exact citations may auto-publish when identifier and target are unambiguous.

relationshipOntology.evidenceRequirement: Statement locator and parsed actor/action/object where applicable.
relationshipOntology.publishRule: Requires confidence and review threshold; compliance-sensitive outputs hold for review.

relationshipOntology.evidenceRequirement: Cross-surface source evidence or bounded inference with rationale.
relationshipOntology.publishRule: Default label is inferred unless the target event/opportunity explicitly cites policy.

relationshipOntology.evidenceRequirement: Supporting and refuting locators where possible; absence claims must state indexed source scope.

decompositionPipeline.qualityGate: Source endpoint reachable, cadence checked, stale signal recorded.
Hash is present, blob write succeeded, fetch status reconciles to SourceRun.
Parser confidence above threshold or review task created.
Required canonical fields present, dates and identifiers validated.
Ambiguous matches create review task; deterministic IDs are stable.
Every edge has evidence, confidence, trust label, created_by, review_state.
Composite score dimensions stored, not just a single opaque model score.
High-impact, inferred, restricted, lifecycle, and low-confidence items are reviewed or held.
Schema version, generated_at, freshness, provenance, and deterministic ordering are included.

sampleGraph.title: Governance Artifact Authority Chain
sampleGraph.description: Trace how a legal section propagates down into implementing issuances, command policy, local procedures, SSP/control narratives, STIG/configuration baselines, and authorization evidence; then run the chain back upward from Echelon 4.
traceGuidance: Downward trace: start at a law section, split into codified text or statutory notes, then follow implementing regulations, memoranda, standards, department issuances, service issuances, command policy, local procedure, SSP/control implementation, configuration baseline, and evidence.

Upward trace: start at an Echelon 4 SOP, SSP control narrative, STIG checklist, topology, POA&M, SAR, or authorization package, then walk through mapped control, command instruction, service policy, DoD issuance, government-wide authority, U.S. Code section, and originating public law section.
Not every public law section becomes U.S. Code text; uncodified statutory notes can still create real tasks, deadlines, reports, pilots, or implementation duties.

The lower layer is not one universal document type. It is usually a bundle: command orders/SOPs, security plans, control mappings, configuration standards, topology/connection packages, test evidence, POA&Ms, and authorization decisions.

traceScenarios.title: Law section to local control
traceScenarios.useCase: What changed downstream because this NDAA section was enacted?
expectedPath: Local SOP / SSP Control / STIG Baseline

traceScenarios.title: SSP control to statute
traceScenarios.useCase: Why does this SSP control narrative, local control, or technical implementation rule exist, and which higher authority supports it?

traceScenarios.title: Evidence package audit trail
traceScenarios.useCase: Can this test, approval, POA&M item, SAR finding, ADD, or screenshot be tied back to an authoritative requirement?

traceScenarios.title: Acquisition flowdown
traceScenarios.useCase: How does statutory or regulatory language become a supplier, contract, or program requirement?

relationshipTypes.label: Amends
relationshipTypes.meaning: A law section modifies an existing statutory section or legal text.
relationshipTypes.evidenceRule: Requires amendatory text plus target citation or source credit.
relationshipTypes.reviewRule: Review if target section is ambiguous, split across multiple titles, or repealed.

relationshipTypes.label: Authorizes
relationshipTypes.meaning: Higher legal authority enables a regulation, memo, policy, program, or responsibility.
relationshipTypes.evidenceRule: Requires authority citation, source credit, reference list, or explicit implementation language.
relationshipTypes.reviewRule: Review if authority is inferred from subject matter only.

relationshipTypes.label: Delegates Task
relationshipTypes.meaning: An artifact assigns action, deadline, report, pilot, plan, or execution responsibility.
relationshipTypes.evidenceRule: Requires actor, modality, action verb, object, and deadline or condition where available.
relationshipTypes.reviewRule: Review when actor or due date is implied.

relationshipTypes.label: Incorporates
relationshipTypes.meaning: A policy imports a standard, publication, control, or reference into implementation.
relationshipTypes.evidenceRule: Requires reference text, control identifier, or direct incorporation language.
relationshipTypes.reviewRule: Review if the target is only background reference and not normative.

relationshipTypes.label: Implements
relationshipTypes.meaning: A downstream issuance operationalizes higher-level policy or government-wide guidance.
relationshipTypes.evidenceRule: Requires references, purpose, applicability, responsibilities, or procedures tying to source authority.
relationshipTypes.reviewRule: Review if multiple upstream authorities compete.

relationshipTypes.label: Service Adopts
relationshipTypes.meaning: A military service or military department implements DoD-level policy through its own publication system.
relationshipTypes.evidenceRule: Requires service publication identifier, applicability statement, reference to DoD authority, and service owner or proponent.
relationshipTypes.reviewRule: Review when a service publication is only adjacent guidance or when DAF/DON applicability differs by subordinate service.

relationshipTypes.label: Echelon 2 Executes
relationshipTypes.meaning: A major command, field command, systems command, component, or equivalent echelon 2 body translates service policy into executable direction.
relationshipTypes.evidenceRule: Requires command owner, service parent, local applicability, and cited service-level source.
relationshipTypes.reviewRule: Review if a component instruction appears to weaken or bypass service policy.

relationshipTypes.label: Tailors
relationshipTypes.meaning: A command, program, or system artifact narrows a higher requirement into local context.
relationshipTypes.evidenceRule: Requires mapped requirement, owner, local scope, and tailoring rationale.
relationshipTypes.reviewRule: Review if tailoring weakens or conflicts with higher authority.

relationshipTypes.label: Localizes
relationshipTypes.meaning: A local order, SOP, SSP control narrative, checklist, or baseline converts policy into local operating or technical implementation language.
relationshipTypes.evidenceRule: Requires local text plus upstream citation, mapped control, governing instruction, or authorization package locator.
relationshipTypes.reviewRule: Review if the local artifact lacks a cited authority path or weakens a higher-level requirement.

relationshipTypes.label: Verifies
relationshipTypes.meaning: Evidence proves implementation, testing, exception, finding closure, authorization, or continued monitoring.
relationshipTypes.evidenceRule: Requires artifact hash, date, owner, assessment/test/approval record, and linked control or authorization artifact.
relationshipTypes.reviewRule: Review if evidence proves existence but not implementation.

propagationStages.failureMode: The text cannot be trusted later if raw bytes, hash, and source metadata are not preserved.
Analysts confuse an old version, draft, cancellation notice, or temporary message with the current authority.
Whole-document matching hides the exact clause or sentence that created the downstream effect.
A local statement cites a vague instruction but not the exact section, reference, or authority basis.
The graph says something is related, but nobody knows who must do what.
Edges become decorative instead of auditable, publishable claims.
A requirement appears universal when it only applies to a component, activity, program, or system class.
A lower echelon weakens higher authority without review or loses the rationale for local interpretation.
The system can cite policy but cannot prove the policy was implemented.

semanticModel.intent: The authority chain graphic is an orientation snapshot. The durable product model is the set of governance artifacts, statements, claims, references, obligations, relationships, review states, and evidence locators extracted from those artifacts.

claimTypes.meaning: States that an artifact, office, role, or action derives authority from another source.
Defines who, what, where, or under which conditions the artifact applies.
Creates a required action or condition.
Forbids an action, state, configuration, or behavior.
Allows an action under specified conditions.
Assigns authority, responsibility, or execution ownership to another office or echelon.
Creates an absolute, relative, recurring, or event-triggered timing requirement.
Requires a report, certification, plan, briefing, notification, or data submission.
Defines a term, category, role, system boundary, or controlled vocabulary.
Creates status, cancellation, supersession, amendment, expiration, or review lifecycle meaning.
Maps policy language to a local implementation artifact, RMF control, SSP narrative, baseline, system boundary, or work product.
Asserts that evidence proves, refutes, tests, authorizes, excepts, or partially satisfies a claim.
Connects a governance artifact to an event, opportunity, risk, program, cost, market signal, or operational effect.

referenceTypes.meaning: Explicit citation to another law, regulation, issuance, section, appendix, form, control, or artifact.
Reference that establishes legal, policy, or delegated authority.
Reference that imports a standard, publication, control, clause, or requirement into the artifact.
Reference used to cancel, supersede, amend, replace, expire, or continue another artifact.
Reference that limits scope by organization, system, program, location, personnel, contract, or mission.
Reference to a technical control, SSP implementation statement, security requirement, assessment objective, STIG rule, clause, or implementation measure.
Stable pointer to page, section, paragraph, table, line, byte range, text quote, hash, or review decision supporting a claim.

relationshipPrimitives.meaning: The source relies on target authority.
The source changes target text or status.
The source accepts target requirements into its own governance layer.
The source assigns authority or responsibility to target.
The source creates a task, deliverable, or execution duty.
The source constrains a task by time.
The source adapts target requirement to local context.
The source expresses higher guidance as local operating language.
The source proves or tests target implementation.
The source appears inconsistent with target and requires review.
The source creates a downstream intelligence effect.

nodes.label: NDAA Section
nodes.subtitle: enacted requirement
nodes.summary: A discrete section in an enacted authorization or appropriations law. This is the starting object when analysts ask what downstream policy changed because of this law.
questions: Did this section amend existing law or create freestanding direction?
Who is assigned action and by when?

nodes.label: Public Law
nodes.subtitle: slip law / Stat.
nodes.summary: The authenticated law package and Statutes at Large citation that anchors the legal text and preserves the enacted source.
questions: Which sections are codified, uncodified, repealed, or amended?

nodes.label: U.S.C. Section
nodes.subtitle: positive law text
nodes.summary: A codified section of the U.S. Code used for stable long-lived statutory lineage, source credits, amendment history, and cross references.
questions: Which public law section created or amended this U.S.C. section?

nodes.label: Statutory Note
nodes.subtitle: uncodified task
nodes.summary: Freestanding statutory direction that may not become a U.S.C. section but still drives reporting, policy, funding, or implementation tasks.
questions: Does this note generate an obligation even if it is not codified?

nodes.label: CFR / eCFR
nodes.subtitle: regulatory rule
nodes.summary: Regulatory text that implements statutory authority through agency rulemaking and can be traced through Federal Register dockets and CFR authority notes.
questions: Which statutory authority and Federal Register action support the rule?

nodes.label: OMB Memo
nodes.subtitle: gov-wide policy
nodes.summary: Government-wide management or budget policy that translates statutory direction into agency implementation expectations.
questions: Which agencies are assigned action and what evidence proves completion?

nodes.label: NIST SP
nodes.subtitle: standard / control
nodes.summary: A technical standard, control catalog, profile, or publication that becomes an implementation reference for cyber, privacy, AI, and risk-management policy.
questions: Which control or requirement is incorporated into DoD or local policy?

nodes.label: FAR / DFARS
nodes.subtitle: acquisition clause
nodes.summary: Acquisition rules, clauses, and provisions that turn statutory or policy drivers into procurement requirements and opportunity language.
questions: Does this requirement appear in solicitations or contract deliverables?

nodes.label: DoDD
nodes.subtitle: department policy
nodes.summary: A DoD Directive establishes broad policy, assigns responsibilities, and delegates authority across the Department.
questions: Which offices own policy execution and review?

nodes.label: DoDI
nodes.subtitle: instruction
nodes.summary: A DoD Instruction implements policy, assigns responsibilities, and establishes procedures or requirements for components.
questions: Which component actions and reporting requirements are created?

nodes.label: DoDM
nodes.subtitle: manual / volume
nodes.summary: A DoD Manual or volume provides detailed procedures, formats, roles, and operating rules under a directive or instruction.
questions: What local procedure or artifact must align to this manual?

nodes.label: Army
nodes.subtitle: AR / DA Pam / EXORD
nodes.summary: Army service implementation converts DoD policy into Department of the Army publications, orders, command supplements, and unit-level execution.
questions: Which Army regulation or order implements the DoD requirement?
Which command or unit supplement carries it further?

serviceImplementations.downstream: Army command regulations, supplements, orders, SOPs
unit TTPs, playbooks, training and inspection artifacts
command execution orders and local action trackers

nodes.label: Navy
nodes.subtitle: SECNAV / OPNAV
nodes.summary: Navy implementation usually passes through Department of the Navy issuances, OPNAV instructions or manuals, message traffic, and type commander or systems-command policy.
questions: Is the controlling Navy artifact department-level, OPNAV-level, message tasking, or command policy?
Which Echelon 2 command owns implementation?

serviceImplementations.downstream: Navy and Marine Corps implementation issuances
fleet, type commander, systems command, region, and activity policy
command instructions, local SOPs, system control statements

nodes.label: Marine Corps
nodes.subtitle: MCO / MARADMIN
nodes.summary: Marine Corps implementation carries DoD or DON policy through Marine Corps Orders, bulletins, messages, and command-level orders.
questions: Does the Marine Corps order implement DON policy directly or via Navy-level instruction?
Which MEF, MARFOR, installation, or command order localizes it?

serviceImplementations.downstream: MARFOR, MEF, installation, and unit orders
time-sensitive command actions and local task trackers

nodes.label: Air Force
nodes.subtitle: DAFI / AFI / AFMAN
nodes.summary: Air Force implementation flows through Department of the Air Force and Air Force publications, manuals, guidance memoranda, supplements, and major command policy.
questions: Is the requirement departmental, Air Force-specific, a manual procedure, or temporary guidance?
Which MAJCOM, NAF, wing, or squadron supplement implements it?

serviceImplementations.downstream: MAJCOM, NAF, wing, and unit supplements
rapid updates before formal publication revision

nodes.label: Space Force
nodes.subtitle: USSF / DAF pubs
nodes.summary: Space Force implementation uses USSF-specific publications where available and Department of the Air Force publications where they apply to USSF organizations.
questions: Is the requirement implemented by a USSF-specific publication or a DAF publication with USSF applicability?
Which field command, delta, garrison, or unit policy carries it down?

serviceImplementations.family: Department of the Air Force shared policy
serviceImplementations.downstream: USSF applicability clauses, supplements, and local procedures
mission system statements and evidence packages

nodes.label: Ech. 2 Policy
nodes.subtitle: major command / component
nodes.summary: Major command, component, field command, systems command, agency, or equivalent echelon 2 governance that converts service policy into executable direction for subordinate commands and program offices.
questions: Which service implementation path produced this echelon 2 policy?
Which lower echelon artifacts prove local implementation?

nodes.label: Program Guide
nodes.subtitle: implementation guide
nodes.summary: A program office guide, handbook, or playbook that translates governance into project, system, or capability implementation patterns.
questions: Which work products and evidence packages are expected?

nodes.label: Ech. 3 Instr.
nodes.subtitle: command policy
nodes.summary: Command or activity-level instruction that delegates responsibilities and creates local operating rules under higher-level issuances.
questions: Which Echelon 4 orders, SOPs, SSP controls, checklists, baselines, and authorization artifacts must conform?

nodes.label: Control Map
nodes.subtitle: authority matrix
nodes.summary: A mapping matrix that ties external authority, service policy, local requirements, RMF controls, system boundaries, implementation artifacts, and evidence together.
questions: Can every SSP control, baseline, and evidence item be traced to higher authority?

nodes.label: Local Order / SOP
nodes.subtitle: command procedure
nodes.summary: A command-issued order, local instruction, SOP, desktop guide, checklist, or standard work package that describes how governed work is performed at the activity, installation, unit, or detachment level.
questions: Which higher-level authority justifies this local procedure, and which system/control artifact proves it?

nodes.label: SSP Control
nodes.subtitle: control narrative
nodes.summary: A system security plan control implementation statement or control narrative that explains how the system, enclave, or program satisfies a selected RMF/NIST control, including inherited controls, responsible owners, boundaries, and planned implementation.
questions: Which upstream control, policy, and authority path supports this implementation statement?

nodes.label: Auth. Evidence
nodes.subtitle: SAR / POA&M / ADD
nodes.summary: Security assessment reports, POA&Ms, authorization decision documents, test results, approvals, logs, screenshots, scan outputs, connection artifacts, and other proof that the local requirement is implemented, accepted, excepted, or deficient.
questions: Does evidence prove implementation, document accepted risk, or merely cite the policy?

nodes.label: STIG / Baseline
nodes.subtitle: configuration rule
nodes.summary: A technical implementation artifact such as a DISA STIG checklist, configuration baseline, firewall rule standard, scan result, topology constraint, or hardened image profile that turns the SSP/control narrative into testable system state.
questions: Does this baseline actually enforce the control, and where are exceptions or deviations approved?

edges.label: enacted in
edges.evidence: The section is located inside the authenticated public law or Statutes at Large package.

edges.label: amends
edges.evidence: Amendatory language and U.S. Code source credit identify the statutory section changed by the law.

edges.label: creates note
edges.evidence: Freestanding tasking, pilot, reporting, or transition language remains attached to the public law section.

edges.label: authorizes
edges.evidence: CFR authority notes and Federal Register rulemaking metadata cite statutory authorities.

edges.label: drives memo
edges.evidence: Government-wide memoranda cite statutory authority or implementation responsibilities.

edges.label: assigns task
edges.evidence: Statutory notes often create deadlines, plans, pilots, briefings, or implementation reporting.

edges.label: incorporates
edges.evidence: OMB policy can reference NIST publications, controls, profiles, or technical standards.

edges.label: drives clause
edges.evidence: Acquisition rulemaking and clauses can be traced to statutory requirements or NDAA sections.

edges.label: constrains
edges.evidence: Department policy must account for applicable government-wide regulation.

edges.label: implemented by
edges.evidence: DoD issuances reference government-wide guidance, standards, and implementation memoranda.

edges.label: control basis
edges.evidence: DoD cyber and risk policy incorporates technical standards and control language.

edges.label: procurement flow
edges.evidence: Contract clauses and provisions shape program guidance, deliverables, and supplier obligations.

edges.label: implements policy
edges.evidence: Instructions implement or supplement directive-level policy and assigned responsibilities.

edges.label: procedures
edges.evidence: Manuals or volumes provide detailed procedures for an instruction.

edges.label: service adopts
edges.evidence: Army regulations, directives, orders, or pamphlets can implement DoD policy and assign Army responsibilities.

edges.label: service adopts
edges.evidence: Department of the Navy and OPNAV issuances can implement DoD policy and assign Navy responsibilities.

edges.label: service adopts
edges.evidence: Marine Corps orders, bulletins, messages, and command orders can implement DoD or DON policy.

edges.label: service adopts
edges.evidence: Department of the Air Force and Air Force publications can implement DoD policy and assign Air Force responsibilities.

edges.label: service adopts
edges.evidence: USSF-specific publications and DAF publications with USSF applicability can implement DoD policy for Space Force organizations.

edges.label: procedures flow
edges.evidence: Service manuals, pamphlets, supplements, and instructions can tailor DoD procedural manuals.

edges.label: procedures flow
edges.evidence: Navy instructions and manuals tailor DoD procedures for Navy commands and activities.

edges.label: procedures flow
edges.evidence: Air Force and Department of the Air Force manuals can tailor DoD procedural manuals.

edges.label: component executes
edges.evidence: Army major command, ASCC, DRU, installation, or program policies execute Army service-level publications.

edges.label: component executes
edges.evidence: Fleet, type commander, systems command, region, or activity policies execute Navy service-level publications.

edges.label: component executes
edges.evidence: MARFOR, MEF, installation, or command policies execute Marine Corps service-level publications.

edges.label: component executes
edges.evidence: MAJCOM, NAF, center, wing, or program policies execute Air Force service-level publications.

edges.label: component executes
edges.evidence: Field command, delta, garrison, unit, or program policies execute Space Force service-level publications.

edges.label: delegates
edges.evidence: Command-level guidance inherits service and echelon 2 policy and translates it to local governance.

edges.label: tailors
edges.evidence: Program or system guides tailor command policy into implementation patterns.

edges.label: maps controls
edges.evidence: Control matrices map policy requirements to implementation owners, systems, and evidence.

edges.label: governs work
edges.evidence: Echelon 3 command guidance governs the echelon 4 SOP, checklist, or local procedure.

edges.label: implements control
edges.evidence: SSP control narratives should cite mapped controls, system boundaries, and authority lineage.

edges.label: feeds control
edges.evidence: Local SOPs, orders, and desktop procedures describe operating processes that support SSP control implementation statements.

edges.label: verified by
edges.evidence: Assessment evidence, findings, POA&Ms, and authorization decisions prove whether the baseline or control is implemented, excepted, or deficient.

edges.label: technicalizes
edges.evidence: Control narratives become testable through STIG rules, configuration baselines, firewall standards, topology constraints, or scan criteria.

edges.label: assessed by
edges.evidence: Security plans, control narratives, SARs, POA&Ms, and authorization decisions are linked as the RMF authorization package.

edges.label: maps rule
edges.evidence: Control maps can bind upstream requirements directly to STIG checks, baselines, and system configuration rules.

storageMapping.rule: Immutable artifacts and generated file outputs.
Derived, rebuildable from PostgreSQL plus Blob.

storageMapping.store: Graph View or Optional Graph Database
storageMapping.rule: Derived view in MVP; dedicated graph database only after benchmark.
